member.name: T:MyOrg.Elements.Configuration.IServiceRegistry`1
member.summary: Provides named service instance resolution for polymorphic service registrations.
member.typeparam: The service abstraction type.

member.name: P:MyOrg.Elements.Configuration.IServiceRegistry`1.Default
member.summary: Gets the default service instance.
member.exception: Thrown when no default service instance is registered.

member.name: P:MyOrg.Elements.Configuration.IServiceRegistry`1.Names
member.summary: Gets the names of all registered service instances.

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Configuration.IServiceRegistry`1.Get(System.String)
member.summary: Gets a named service instance.
member.param: The instance name.
member.returns: The service instance.
member.exception: Thrown when is null, empty, or whitespace.
Thrown when no service instance with the specified is registered.

example.code: IMessageBus internalBus = registry.Get("Internal");

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Configuration.IServiceRegistry`1.TryGet(System.String)
member.summary: Attempts to get a named service instance, returning null when no instance with the specified is registered.
member.param: The instance name.
member.returns: The service instance if registered; otherwise, null.
member.exception: Thrown when is null, empty, or whitespace.
member.remarks: Returns a nullable reference rather than using an out parameter to remain compatible with the covariant type parameter on this interface.

member.name: T:MyOrg.Elements.Configuration.PolymorphicServiceBuilder`1
member.summary: Builder for registering provider implementations in a polymorphic service configuration.
member.typeparam: The service abstraction type.

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Configuration.PolymorphicServiceBuilder`1.AddProvider``2(System.String,Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.ServiceLifetime)
member.summary: Registers a provider with strongly-typed options that are auto-bound from the Settings configuration section. The implementation is constructed via ActivatorUtilities.CreateInstance, so additional constructor dependencies are resolved from DI automatically.
member.typeparam: The concrete implementation type.
The options type, bound from the Settings section.
member.param: The provider name as it appears in the "Provider" configuration value.
The DI lifetime for instances of this provider. Defaults to .
member.returns: The builder for chaining.
member.exception: Thrown when is , empty, or whitespace.

example.code: builder.AddProvider<KafkaMessageBus, KafkaOptions>("Kafka");

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Configuration.PolymorphicServiceBuilder`1.AddProvider``1(System.String,Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.ServiceLifetime)
member.summary: Registers a provider with no options. The implementation is constructed via ActivatorUtilities.CreateInstance, so constructor dependencies are resolved from DI.
member.typeparam: The concrete implementation type.
member.param: The provider name as it appears in the "Provider" configuration value.
The DI lifetime for instances of this provider. Defaults to .
member.returns: The builder for chaining.
member.exception: Thrown when is , empty, or whitespace.

example.code: builder.AddProvider<NoopMessageBus>("Noop");

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Configuration.PolymorphicServiceBuilder`1.GetRegistration(System.String)
member.summary: Gets the registration for the specified provider name.

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Configuration.PolymorphicServiceBuilder`1.GetAllProviderNames
member.summary: Gets all registered provider names.

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Configuration.PolymorphicServiceBuilder`1.RegisterKeyedService(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection,System.String,System.String,Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration.IConfigurationSection)
member.summary: Registers a provider implementation as a keyed service in the service collection. Options are configured using the service key as the named options key.
member.param: The service collection.
The provider name (as registered via ).
The DI service key for this instance.
Optional configuration section containing provider settings.
member.exception: Thrown when is .
Thrown when or is , empty, or whitespace.
Thrown when no provider with has been registered on this builder.

example.code: builder.RegisterKeyedService( services, providerName: "Kafka", serviceKey: "Internal", settingsSection: configuration.GetSection("Messaging:Internal:Settings"));

member.name: T:MyOrg.Elements.Configuration.PolymorphicServiceCollectionExtensions
member.summary: Extension methods for registering polymorphic services from configuration.

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Configuration.PolymorphicServiceCollectionExtensions.AddPolymorphicService``1(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection,Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration.IConfiguration,System.String,System.Action{MyOrg.Elements.Configuration.PolymorphicServiceBuilder{``0}})
member.typeparam: The service abstraction type.
member.param: The service collection.
The configuration root.
The configuration section path (e.g., "Messaging").
Action to register provider implementations.
member.returns: The service collection for chaining.
member.exception: Thrown when or is .
Thrown when is , empty, or whitespace.
Thrown when the configured Provider name has not been registered, when the configured section is missing, or when a multi-provider configuration cannot resolve a default provider.

member.name: T:MyOrg.Elements.Configuration.PolymorphicServiceRegistration
member.summary: Captures the registration metadata for a single provider in a polymorphic service configuration.

member.name: P:MyOrg.Elements.Configuration.PolymorphicServiceRegistration.ConfigureOptions
member.summary: Action that calls services.Configure<TOptions>(name, section). Null when the provider has no options type.

member.name: P:MyOrg.Elements.Configuration.PolymorphicServiceRegistration.Factory
member.summary: Factory that creates the service instance. Parameters: (serviceProvider, instanceName) → service.

member.name: P:MyOrg.Elements.Configuration.PolymorphicServiceRegistration.Lifetime
member.summary: The DI lifetime for this provider's service instances.

member.name: T:MyOrg.Elements.Configuration.ServiceRegistry`1
member.summary: Default implementation of backed by keyed DI services.

member.name: T:MyOrg.Elements.Guards.CollectionGuardClauseExtensions
member.summary: Guard-clause extensions for -shaped arguments.
member.remarks: The methods are generic on the concrete collection type (any reference type implementing ) rather than on a fixed IEnumerable<T> carrier. This lets callers pass a value typed as List<int>, int[], Dictionary<K,V>, etc. directly — including var-typed locals and callers compiled with <Nullable>disable</Nullable> — without forcing them to widen to IEnumerable<T>?. Generics are invariant on the carrier struct, so a fixed GuardClause<IEnumerable<T>?> parameter would not accept a GuardClause<List<T>>; the open generic shape avoids that.

example.code: IReadOnlyList<int> items = Guard.Against(items).NullOrEmpty().MaxCount(100).Value; string[] tags = Guard.Against(tags).MinCount(1).ExactCount(3).Value;

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Guards.CollectionGuardClauseExtensions.NullOrEmpty``1(MyOrg.Elements.Guards.GuardClause{``0})
member.summary: Throws when the collection is null or contains no elements.
member.typeparam: The concrete collection type, which must implement .
member.param: The wrapping the collection to check.
member.returns: The input unchanged, enabling further chaining.
member.exception: Thrown when is null.
Thrown when contains no elements.

example.code: IReadOnlyList<int> items = Guard.Against(items).NullOrEmpty().Value;

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Guards.CollectionGuardClauseExtensions.MinCount``1(MyOrg.Elements.Guards.GuardClause{``0},System.Int32)
member.summary: Throws when the collection is null or contains fewer than elements.
member.typeparam: The concrete collection type, which must implement .
member.param: The wrapping the collection to check.
The minimum number of elements required (inclusive). Must be non-negative.
member.returns: The input unchanged, enabling further chaining.
member.exception: Thrown when is null.
Thrown when is negative.
Thrown when the collection has fewer than elements.
member.remarks: Counting prefers when available (O(1)); otherwise iterates lazily up to .

example.code: IReadOnlyList<int> items = Guard.Against(items).MinCount(2).Value;

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Guards.CollectionGuardClauseExtensions.MaxCount``1(MyOrg.Elements.Guards.GuardClause{``0},System.Int32)
member.summary: Throws when the collection is null or contains more than elements.
member.typeparam: The concrete collection type, which must implement .
member.param: The wrapping the collection to check.
The maximum number of elements allowed (inclusive). Must be non-negative.
member.returns: The input unchanged, enabling further chaining.
member.exception: Thrown when is null.
Thrown when is negative.
Thrown when the collection has more than elements.
member.remarks: Counting prefers when available (O(1)); otherwise iterates lazily up to + 1.

example.code: IReadOnlyList<int> items = Guard.Against(items).MaxCount(100).Value;

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Guards.CollectionGuardClauseExtensions.ExactCount``1(MyOrg.Elements.Guards.GuardClause{``0},System.Int32)
member.summary: Throws when the collection is null or does not contain exactly elements.
member.typeparam: The concrete collection type, which must implement .
member.param: The wrapping the collection to check.
The required element count. Must be non-negative.
member.returns: The input unchanged, enabling further chaining.
member.exception: Thrown when is null.
Thrown when is negative.
Thrown when the collection has a different number of elements than .
member.remarks: Counting prefers when available (O(1)); otherwise iterates lazily up to + 1.

example.code: IReadOnlyList<string> coords = Guard.Against(coords).ExactCount(3).Value;

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Guards.CollectionGuardClauseExtensions.CountAtLeast(System.Collections.IEnumerable,System.Int32)
member.summary: Counts elements in , short-circuiting when the count reaches . Returns the actual count when below the ceiling, or when at or above it. Uses O(1) when available.

member.name: T:MyOrg.Elements.Guards.ComparableGuardClauseExtensions
member.summary: Guard-clause extensions for arguments implementing .

example.code: int age = Guard.Against(age).LessThan(0).GreaterThan(150).Value; decimal price = Guard.Against(price).OutOfRange(0m, 9_999.99m).Value;

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Guards.ComparableGuardClauseExtensions.LessThan``1(MyOrg.Elements.Guards.GuardClause{``0},``0)
member.summary: Throws when the value is less than .
member.typeparam: The comparable type, which must implement .
member.param: The wrapping the value to check.
The exclusive lower bound to compare against.
member.returns: The input unchanged, enabling further chaining.
member.exception: Thrown when is less than .

example.code: int age = Guard.Against(age).LessThan(0).Value;

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Guards.ComparableGuardClauseExtensions.LessThanOrEqualTo``1(MyOrg.Elements.Guards.GuardClause{``0},``0)
member.summary: Throws when the value is less than or equal to .
member.typeparam: The comparable type, which must implement .
member.param: The wrapping the value to check.
The value that the guarded value must be strictly greater than.
member.returns: The input unchanged, enabling further chaining.
member.exception: Thrown when is less than or equal to .

example.code: int priority = Guard.Against(priority).LessThanOrEqualTo(0).Value;

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Guards.ComparableGuardClauseExtensions.GreaterThan``1(MyOrg.Elements.Guards.GuardClause{``0},``0)
member.summary: Throws when the value is greater than .
member.typeparam: The comparable type, which must implement .
member.param: The wrapping the value to check.
The exclusive upper bound to compare against.
member.returns: The input unchanged, enabling further chaining.
member.exception: Thrown when is greater than .

example.code: int age = Guard.Against(age).GreaterThan(150).Value;

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Guards.ComparableGuardClauseExtensions.GreaterThanOrEqualTo``1(MyOrg.Elements.Guards.GuardClause{``0},``0)
member.summary: Throws when the value is greater than or equal to .
member.typeparam: The comparable type, which must implement .
member.param: The wrapping the value to check.
The value that the guarded value must be strictly less than.
member.returns: The input unchanged, enabling further chaining.
member.exception: Thrown when is greater than or equal to .

example.code: int index = Guard.Against(index).GreaterThanOrEqualTo(list.Count).Value;

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Guards.ComparableGuardClauseExtensions.OutOfRange``1(MyOrg.Elements.Guards.GuardClause{``0},``0,``0)
member.summary: Throws when the value falls outside the inclusive range [, ].
member.typeparam: The comparable type, which must implement .
member.param: The wrapping the value to check.
The inclusive lower bound of the acceptable range.
The inclusive upper bound of the acceptable range.
member.returns: The input unchanged, enabling further chaining.
member.exception: Thrown when is less than or greater than .

example.code: int percentage = Guard.Against(percentage).OutOfRange(0, 100).Value;

member.name: T:MyOrg.Elements.Guards.DateTimeGuardClauseExtensions
member.summary: Guard-clause extensions for and arguments.

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Guards.DateTimeGuardClauseExtensions.InThePast(MyOrg.Elements.Guards.GuardClause{System.DateTime})
member.summary: Throws when the value is in the past relative to "now".
member.remarks: Uses when is or ; only compares against . Treating Unspecified as UTC avoids latent time-zone bugs when values originate from databases or serialization formats that drop the kind.
member.param: The wrapping the to check.
member.returns: The input unchanged, enabling further chaining.
member.exception: Thrown when is earlier than the current date and time.

example.code: DateTime scheduledAt = Guard.Against(scheduledAt).InThePast().Value;

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Guards.DateTimeGuardClauseExtensions.InThePast(MyOrg.Elements.Guards.GuardClause{System.DateTimeOffset})
member.summary: Throws when the offset-aware value is in the past relative to .
member.param: The wrapping the to check.
member.returns: The input unchanged, enabling further chaining.
member.exception: Thrown when is earlier than .

example.code: DateTimeOffset expiresAt = Guard.Against(expiresAt).InThePast().Value;

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Guards.DateTimeGuardClauseExtensions.InTheFuture(MyOrg.Elements.Guards.GuardClause{System.DateTime})
member.summary: Throws when the value is in the future relative to "now".
member.remarks: Uses when is or ; only compares against .
member.param: The wrapping the to check.
member.returns: The input unchanged, enabling further chaining.
member.exception: Thrown when is later than the current date and time.

example.code: DateTime dateOfBirth = Guard.Against(dateOfBirth).InTheFuture().Value;

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Guards.DateTimeGuardClauseExtensions.InTheFuture(MyOrg.Elements.Guards.GuardClause{System.DateTimeOffset})
member.summary: Throws when the offset-aware value is in the future relative to .
member.param: The wrapping the to check.
member.returns: The input unchanged, enabling further chaining.
member.exception: Thrown when is later than .

example.code: DateTimeOffset createdAt = Guard.Against(createdAt).InTheFuture().Value;

member.name: T:MyOrg.Elements.Guards.EnumGuardClauseExtensions
member.summary: Guard-clause extensions for arguments.

example.code: OrderStatus status = Guard.Against(status).InvalidEnum().Value; DayOfWeek day = Guard.Against(day).InvalidEnum().Value;

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Guards.EnumGuardClauseExtensions.InvalidEnum``1(MyOrg.Elements.Guards.GuardClause{``0})
member.summary: Throws when the enum value is not a defined member of .
member.typeparam: The enum type whose defined members are validated against.
member.param: The wrapping the enum value to check.
member.returns: The input unchanged, enabling further chaining.
member.exception: Thrown when is not a defined member of .

example.code: Status status = Guard.Against(status).InvalidEnum().Value;

member.name: T:MyOrg.Elements.Guards.Guard
member.summary: Entry point for fluent guard-clause checks against method/constructor arguments.

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Guards.Guard.Against``1(``0,System.String)
member.summary: Begins a fluent guard-clause chain for the supplied . The argument's source-text expression is captured automatically and used as the parameter name on any thrown (or derived type).
member.typeparam: Type of the value being guarded. Inferred from .
member.param: The value to guard against invalid states.
Captured automatically via . Callers should rarely supply this explicitly.
member.returns: A wrapping , ready for chaining further guard checks.

example.code: string name = Guard.Against(name).NullOrEmpty().LongerThan(100).Value;

member.name: T:MyOrg.Elements.Guards.GuardClause`1
member.summary: Lightweight, allocation-free carrier used by the fluent API. Each guard extension validates the wrapped and returns a so that further checks can be chained.
member.typeparam: Type of the value being guarded.

member.name: P:MyOrg.Elements.Guards.GuardClause`1.Value
member.summary: Gets the value being guarded.

member.name: P:MyOrg.Elements.Guards.GuardClause`1.ParameterName
member.summary: Gets the captured parameter name used when throwing argument exceptions. Always non-null; if the caller supplied null (or the capture failed) this is normalised to "value" at construction time so downstream guard extensions never have to deal with a null parameter name.

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Guards.GuardClause`1.op_Implicit(MyOrg.Elements.Guards.GuardClause{`0})~`0
member.summary: Implicitly unwraps the guarded value, allowing terminal use of the chain in constructors and field initializers without an explicit .Value.
member.param: The whose value is unwrapped.
member.returns: The guarded value contained in .

example.code: // Implicit conversion eliminates the explicit .Value at the end of the chain: string name = Guard.Against(rawName).NullOrEmpty().LongerThan(100);

member.name: T:MyOrg.Elements.Guards.NumericGuardClauseExtensions
member.summary: Guard-clause extensions for numeric types implementing (covers , , , , , , , , etc.).

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Guards.NumericGuardClauseExtensions.Negative``1(MyOrg.Elements.Guards.GuardClause{``0})
member.summary: Throws when the number is negative.
member.typeparam: The numeric type, which must implement .
member.param: The wrapping the numeric value to check.
member.returns: The input unchanged, enabling further chaining.
member.exception: Thrown when is negative.

example.code: int count = Guard.Against(count).Negative().Value;

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Guards.NumericGuardClauseExtensions.NegativeOrZero``1(MyOrg.Elements.Guards.GuardClause{``0})
member.summary: Throws when the number is negative or zero.
member.typeparam: The numeric type, which must implement .
member.param: The wrapping the numeric value to check.
member.returns: The input unchanged, enabling further chaining.
member.exception: Thrown when is negative or zero.

example.code: int quantity = Guard.Against(quantity).NegativeOrZero().Value;

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Guards.NumericGuardClauseExtensions.Positive``1(MyOrg.Elements.Guards.GuardClause{``0})
member.summary: Throws when the number is positive (non-zero).
member.typeparam: The numeric type, which must implement .
member.param: The wrapping the numeric value to check.
member.returns: The input unchanged, enabling further chaining.
member.exception: Thrown when is positive and non-zero.

example.code: int offset = Guard.Against(offset).Positive().Value;

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Guards.NumericGuardClauseExtensions.Zero``1(MyOrg.Elements.Guards.GuardClause{``0})
member.summary: Throws when the number is zero.
member.typeparam: The numeric type, which must implement .
member.param: The wrapping the numeric value to check.
member.returns: The input unchanged, enabling further chaining.
member.exception: Thrown when is zero.

example.code: int divisor = Guard.Against(divisor).Zero().Value;

member.name: T:MyOrg.Elements.Guards.ObjectGuardClauseExtensions
member.summary: Guard-clause extensions that apply to any reference- or value-typed argument: null checks (including ) and default-value checks.

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Guards.ObjectGuardClauseExtensions.Null``1(MyOrg.Elements.Guards.GuardClause{``0})
member.summary: Throws when the guarded reference is null.
member.typeparam: The reference type of the guarded value.
member.param: The wrapping the value to check.
member.returns: The input unchanged, enabling further chaining.
member.exception: Thrown when is null.
member.remarks: Carrier annotation is preserved; the runtime check is what matters here.

example.code: string name = Guard.Against(name).Null().LongerThan(100).Value;

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Guards.ObjectGuardClauseExtensions.Null``1(MyOrg.Elements.Guards.GuardClause{System.Nullable{``0}})
member.summary: Throws when the guarded has no value. Refines the carrier to a non-nullable .
member.typeparam: The underlying value type of the nullable guarded value.
member.param: The wrapping the nullable value to check.
member.returns: A non-nullable wrapping the unwrapped value, enabling further chaining.
member.exception: Thrown when has no value.

example.code: int value = Guard.Against(nullableInt).Null().Negative().Value;

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Guards.ObjectGuardClauseExtensions.Default``1(MyOrg.Elements.Guards.GuardClause{``0})
member.summary: Throws when the value equals default(T).
member.typeparam: The value type of the guarded value.
member.param: The wrapping the value to check.
member.returns: The input unchanged, enabling further chaining.
member.exception: Thrown when equals default(T).
member.remarks: Constrained to value types since default for a reference type is null, which is the responsibility of .

example.code: Guid id = Guard.Against(id).Default().Value;

member.name: T:MyOrg.Elements.Guards.StringGuardClauseExtensions
member.summary: Guard-clause extensions for arguments.
member.remarks:
Each null-checking method is provided in two shapes:
a primary overload that accepts GuardClause<string?> for callers whose value is statically nullable;
a complementary overload (using the in modifier so the IL signature differs) that accepts GuardClause<string> for callers passing a statically non-nullable string — most commonly literals or var-typed locals after flow-narrowing.
This pair keeps both nullable-aware and null-oblivious callers free of the CS8620 generic-struct-invariance warning.

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Guards.StringGuardClauseExtensions.NullOrEmpty(MyOrg.Elements.Guards.GuardClause{System.String})
member.summary: Throws when the string is null or empty, returning a non-nullable carrier on success.
member.param: The wrapping the nullable string to check.
member.returns: A non-nullable wrapping the validated string, enabling further chaining.
member.exception: Thrown when is null.
Thrown when is an empty string.

example.code: string name = Guard.Against(name).NullOrEmpty().LongerThan(100).Value;

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Guards.StringGuardClauseExtensions.NullOrEmpty(MyOrg.Elements.Guards.GuardClause{System.String}@)
member.summary: Convenience overload that accepts a non-nullable carrier (e.g. for literal strings or flow-narrowed locals). Uses in + to coexist with and to be selected when the call site has a non-nullable carrier (avoiding CS8620 invariance warnings). Includes a defensive null check in case nullability annotations are circumvented.
member.param: The wrapping the non-nullable string to check.
member.returns: The input unchanged, enabling further chaining.
member.exception: Thrown when is null despite the non-nullable annotation.
Thrown when is an empty string.

example.code: string name = Guard.Against(name).NullOrEmpty().LongerThan(100).Value;

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Guards.StringGuardClauseExtensions.NullOrWhiteSpace(MyOrg.Elements.Guards.GuardClause{System.String})
member.summary: Throws when the string is null, empty, or whitespace, returning a non-nullable carrier on success.
member.param: The wrapping the nullable string to check.
member.returns: A non-nullable wrapping the validated string, enabling further chaining.
member.exception: Thrown when is null.
Thrown when is empty or contains only whitespace.

example.code: string description = Guard.Against(description).NullOrWhiteSpace().LongerThan(500).Value;

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Guards.StringGuardClauseExtensions.NullOrWhiteSpace(MyOrg.Elements.Guards.GuardClause{System.String}@)
member.summary: Convenience overload accepting a non-nullable carrier; see for full documentation.
member.param: The wrapping the non-nullable string to check.
member.returns: The input unchanged, enabling further chaining.
member.exception: Thrown when is null despite the non-nullable annotation.
Thrown when is empty or contains only whitespace.

example.code: string description = Guard.Against(description).NullOrWhiteSpace().LongerThan(500).Value;

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Guards.StringGuardClauseExtensions.Empty(MyOrg.Elements.Guards.GuardClause{System.String})
member.summary: Throws when the non-null string is empty.
member.param: The wrapping the non-null string to check.
member.returns: The input unchanged, enabling further chaining.
member.exception: Thrown when is an empty string.

example.code: string name = Guard.Against(name).Empty().LongerThan(100).Value;

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Guards.StringGuardClauseExtensions.LongerThan(MyOrg.Elements.Guards.GuardClause{System.String},System.Int32)
member.summary: Throws when the string length exceeds .
member.param: The wrapping the string to check.
The maximum allowable string length (inclusive).
member.returns: The input unchanged, enabling further chaining.
member.exception: Thrown when the length of exceeds .

example.code: string name = Guard.Against(name).NullOrEmpty().LongerThan(100).Value;

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Guards.StringGuardClauseExtensions.ShorterThan(MyOrg.Elements.Guards.GuardClause{System.String},System.Int32)
member.summary: Throws when the string length is below .
member.param: The wrapping the string to check.
The minimum required string length (inclusive).
member.returns: The input unchanged, enabling further chaining.
member.exception: Thrown when the length of is below .

example.code: string code = Guard.Against(code).NullOrEmpty().ShorterThan(3).LongerThan(10).Value;

member.name: T:MyOrg.Elements.IClock
member.summary: Abstraction over the system clock that exposes only static members, enabling deterministic time-based testing via generic type parameters constrained to .
member.remarks: Production code should depend on a type parameter TClock constrained to (e.g. where TClock : IClock) rather than directly using or so that tests can substitute a fixed-time implementation. Use in production.

member.name: P:MyOrg.Elements.IClock.UtcNow
member.summary: Gets the current UTC time as a with .

member.name: P:MyOrg.Elements.IClock.UtcNowOffset
member.summary: Gets the current UTC time as a .

member.name: T:MyOrg.Elements.Mapping.ITypeMapper`2
member.summary: Maps types from one representation to another.
member.typeparam: The source type to map from.
The destination type to map to.

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Mapping.ITypeMapper`2.Map(`0)
member.summary: Maps an instance of to .
member.param: The source instance to map.
member.returns: The mapped instance of .

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Mapping.ITypeMapper`2.Project(`0,`1)
member.summary: Projects the values from the source instance onto the destination instance.
member.param: The source instance to project from.
The destination instance to project onto.

member.name: T:MyOrg.Elements.Mapping.ITypeMappingService
member.summary: Central service for resolving instances and mapping single objects or collections between type representations.
member.remarks: Registration is an implementation concern and lives on the concrete TypeMappingService (and the service-collection extensions), not on this abstraction.

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Mapping.ITypeMappingService.HasMapper``2
member.summary: Returns when a mapper can be resolved for the → pair, either via an exact match or via the inheritance-hierarchy fallback.
member.typeparam: The source type.
The destination type.
member.returns: when a mapper is registered for the pair (exactly or via fallback); otherwise .

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Mapping.ITypeMappingService.TryGetMapper``2
member.summary: Returns the registered for the pair, or when no mapper has been registered.
member.example:
Given a registered mapper for ProductEntity → ProductDto, the following returns the mapper instance:
mappingService.TryGetMapper<ProductEntity, ProductDto>();
member.typeparam: The source type.
The destination type.
member.returns: The resolved mapper, or when no mapper is registered for the pair.

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Mapping.ITypeMappingService.GetMapper``2
member.summary: Returns the registered for the pair.
member.exception: No mapper is registered for the → pair.
member.typeparam: The source type.
The destination type.
member.returns: The resolved mapper for the pair.

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Mapping.ITypeMappingService.Map``2(``0)
member.summary: Maps to a new instance of using the registered mapper.
member.exception: No mapper is registered for the pair.
member.example:
Given a registered mapper for ProductEntity → ProductDto, the following maps a ProductEntity instance to a new ProductDto instance:
var theDto = mappingService.Map<ProductEntity, ProductDto>(productEntity);
member.typeparam: The source type.
The destination type.
member.param: The source instance to map.
member.returns: A new produced by the registered mapper.

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Mapping.ITypeMappingService.TryMap``2(``0)
member.summary: Attempts to map to a new instance, returning the default value of when no mapper is registered for the pair.
member.typeparam: The source type.
The destination type.
member.param: The source instance to map.
member.returns: The mapped value, or default(TDestination) when no mapper is registered for the → pair.
member.remarks: Use when a missing mapper indicates a programmer error and you want a thrown exception. Use TryMap when the absence of a mapper is an acceptable runtime condition.

example.code: ProductDto? dto = mappingService.TryMap<ProductEntity, ProductDto>(entity); if (dto is not null) { /* … */ }

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Mapping.ITypeMappingService.Project``2(``0,``1)
member.summary: Projects the values of onto an existing instance using the registered mapper.
member.exception: No mapper is registered for the pair.
member.example:
Given a registered mapper for ProductEntity → ProductDto, the following projects a ProductEntity instance onto an existing ProductDto instance:
mappingService.Project<ProductEntity, ProductDto>(productEntity, existingProductDto);
member.typeparam: The source type.
The destination type.
member.param: The source instance to project from.
The existing destination instance to project values onto.

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Mapping.ITypeMappingService.MapCollection``2(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0})
member.summary: Lazily maps each element of to using the registered mapper. The sequence is evaluated on first enumeration.
member.exception: No mapper is registered for the pair.
member.typeparam: The source type.
The destination type.
member.param: The source sequence to map.
member.returns: A lazily-evaluated sequence of mapped values.

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Mapping.ITypeMappingService.MapToList``2(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0})
member.summary: Maps each element of and materializes the result as a .
member.exception: No mapper is registered for the pair.
member.example:
Given a registered mapper for ProductEntity → ProductDto, the following maps a sequence of ProductEntity instances to a List{ProductDto}:
var dtoList = mappingService.MapToList<ProductEntity, ProductDto>(productEntities);
member.typeparam: The source type.
The destination type.
member.param: The source sequence to map.
member.returns: A new containing the mapped values.

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Mapping.ITypeMappingService.MapToReadOnlyList``2(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0})
member.summary: Maps each element of and materializes the result as an .
member.exception: No mapper is registered for the pair.
member.example:
Given a registered mapper for ProductEntity → ProductDto, the following maps a sequence of ProductEntity instances to a IReadOnlyList{ProductDto}:
var dtoList = mappingService.MapToReadOnlyList<ProductEntity, ProductDto>(productEntities);
member.typeparam: The source type.
The destination type.
member.param: The source sequence to map.
member.returns: A read-only list containing the mapped values.

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Mapping.ITypeMappingService.MapToCollection``2(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0})
member.summary: Maps each element of and materializes the result as a .
member.exception: No mapper is registered for the pair.
member.example:
Given a registered mapper for ProductEntity → ProductDto, the following maps a sequence of ProductEntity instances to a Collection{ProductDto}:
var dtoCollection = mappingService.MapToCollection<ProductEntity, ProductDto>(productEntities);
member.typeparam: The source type.
The destination type.
member.param: The source sequence to map.
member.returns: A new containing the mapped values.

member.name: T:MyOrg.Elements.Mapping.TypeMappingService
member.summary: Default implementation backed by an in-memory registry of instances.

item.description: Otherwise, candidates are collected where the registered source type is assignable from TSource and the registered destination type is assignable to TDestination (exploiting the variance of ).
The candidate with the closest source type (fewest inheritance hops from TSource) wins; ties are broken by preferring the most specific destination type (deepest in the inheritance hierarchy).

remarks.para: CLR generic variance is required for the fallback cast, so fallback resolution is only supported when both TSource and TDestination are reference types. Exact matches work for value types as well.

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Mapping.TypeMappingService.#ctor
member.summary: Creates an empty .

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Mapping.TypeMappingService.#ctor(System.Object[])
member.summary: Creates a pre-populated with the supplied .
member.param: Mapper instances to register. Each must implement exactly one closed interface (mappers that implement multiple are registered for each closed variant).
member.exception: Thrown when is .
Thrown when any element of is , or when an element does not implement any closed interfaces.

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Mapping.TypeMappingService.#ctor(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.Object})
member.summary: Creates a pre-populated with the supplied .
member.param: Mapper instances to register.
member.exception: Thrown when is .
Thrown when any element of is , or when an element does not implement any closed interfaces.

example.code: IEnumerable<object> mappers = container.ResolveAll<object>(); var service = new TypeMappingService(mappers);

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Mapping.TypeMappingService.Register``2(MyOrg.Elements.Mapping.ITypeMapper{``0,``1},System.Boolean)
member.summary: Registers an explicit mapper for the → pair.
member.typeparam: Destination type.
Source type.
member.param: The mapper instance.
When , replaces any existing registration for the pair.
member.returns: The same service instance for fluent chaining.
member.exception: Thrown when is .

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Mapping.TypeMappingService.RegisterFromAssembly(System.Reflection.Assembly,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Scans and auto-registers every concrete, non-abstract implementation found within it.
member.param: The assembly to scan.
When , duplicate pairs replace existing registrations.
member.returns: The same service instance for fluent chaining.

example.code: var mappingService = new TypeMappingService() .RegisterFromAssembly(typeof(UserEntityToDtoMapper).Assembly);

member.exception: Thrown when is .

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Mapping.TypeMappingService.RegisterFromAssemblies(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.Reflection.Assembly},System.Boolean)
member.summary: Scans each of and auto-registers every concrete, non-abstract implementation found.
member.param: The assemblies to scan.
When , duplicate pairs replace existing registrations.
member.returns: The same service instance for fluent chaining.

example.code: var mappingService = new TypeMappingService() .RegisterFromAssemblies(new[] { typeof(UserEntityToDtoMapper).Assembly, typeof(ProductEntityToDtoMapper).Assembly });

member.exception: Thrown when is .

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Mapping.TypeMappingService.RegisterFromLoadedAssemblies(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Scans every currently loaded assembly in the current and auto-registers every concrete, non-abstract implementation found. Dynamic assemblies are skipped.
member.param: When , duplicate pairs replace existing registrations.
member.returns: The same service instance for fluent chaining.

example.code: var mappingService = new TypeMappingService() .RegisterFromLoadedAssemblies();

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Mapping.TypeMappingService.RegisterInstance(System.Object,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Registers against every closed interface it implements. Intended for scenarios (e.g., DI-based composition) where the exact closed generic arguments are not known at the call site.
member.param: The mapper instance.
When , replaces any existing registration for the pair.
member.exception: Thrown when does not implement any closed interfaces.
Thrown when is .

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Mapping.TypeMappingService.BuildVarianceAdapter(System.Object,System.Type,System.Type,System.Type,System.Type)
member.summary: Builds a typed adapter that exposes a registered mapper (with types → ) as the requested pair ( → ). The CLR can't supply the cast directly because ITypeMapper is invariant on its destination parameter (Project uses it in an input position).

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Mapping.TypeMappingService.SourceDistance(System.Type,System.Type)
member.summary: Returns the number of inheritance hops from up to . Interfaces are given a large constant offset so that concrete base-class matches are preferred over interface matches.

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Mapping.TypeMappingService.InheritanceDepth(System.Type)
member.summary: Returns a non-negative value approximating how specific (deep in the inheritance hierarchy) is. Higher = more specific.

member.name: T:MyOrg.Elements.Mapping.TypeMappingService.VarianceAdapter`4
member.summary: Adapts an so it can be consumed as an , where derives from and derives from .

remarks.para: Map is always safe: the requested source upcasts to the registered source, and the registered destination upcasts to the requested destination.
Project requires the runtime type of destination to be assignable to . An is thrown otherwise.

member.name: T:MyOrg.Elements.Mapping.TypeMappingServiceCollectionExtensions
member.summary: Service-collection extensions for registering and auto-discovering implementations.

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Mapping.TypeMappingServiceCollectionExtensions.AddTypeMapping(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection,System.Action{MyOrg.Elements.Mapping.TypeMappingService})
member.summary: Registers as a singleton . Mappers registered via , , or are folded in automatically; runs last so it may add or override entries.
member.param: The service collection to register the mapping service with.
Optional callback invoked after DI-discovered mappers have been folded in, allowing additional registration or overrides.
member.returns: The same instance for fluent chaining.
member.exception: Thrown when is .

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Mapping.TypeMappingServiceCollectionExtensions.AddTypeMappersFromAssembly(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection,System.Reflection.Assembly)
member.summary: Discovers every concrete, non-abstract implementation in and registers each as a singleton with the DI container. picks them up automatically.
member.param: The service collection to register mappers with.
The assembly to scan for mapper implementations.
member.returns: The same instance for fluent chaining.
member.exception: Thrown when or is .

example.code: services.AddTypeMappersFromAssembly(typeof(ProductEntityToDtoMapper).Assembly);

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Mapping.TypeMappingServiceCollectionExtensions.AddTypeMappersFromAssemblies(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.Reflection.Assembly})
member.summary: Discovers every concrete, non-abstract implementation across and registers each as a singleton.
member.param: The service collection to register mappers with.
The assemblies to scan for mapper implementations. entries are skipped.
member.returns: The same instance for fluent chaining.
member.exception: Thrown when or is .

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Mapping.TypeMappingServiceCollectionExtensions.AddTypeMappersFromLoadedAssemblies(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection)
member.summary: Discovers mappers across every non-dynamic assembly currently loaded into the .
member.param: The service collection to register mappers with.
member.returns: The same instance for fluent chaining.
member.exception: Thrown when is .

example.code: services.AddTypeMappersFromLoadedAssemblies().AddTypeMapping();

member.name: T:MyOrg.Elements.Mapping.TypeMapperDescriptor
member.summary: Internal descriptor used to surface DI-registered mapper instances to without re-scanning assemblies at runtime.

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Mapping.TypeMapperDescriptor.#ctor(System.Object)
member.summary: Internal descriptor used to surface DI-registered mapper instances to without re-scanning assemblies at runtime.

member.name: T:MyOrg.Elements.Range`1
member.summary: Represents a range of values with a specified start and end.
member.typeparam: The type of the values in the range, which must implement .

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Range`1.#ctor(`0,`0)
member.summary: Initializes a new as an inclusive range.
member.param: The start value of the range.
The end value of the range.
member.exception: Thrown when or is .
Thrown when exceeds .

example.code: var range = new Range<int>(1, 10); bool inRange = range.IsInRange(5); // true

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Range`1.#ctor(`0,`0,MyOrg.Elements.RangeType)
member.summary: Initializes a new with the specified bounds and range type.
member.param: The start value of the range.
The end value of the range.
The type of the range.
member.exception: Thrown when or is .
Thrown when exceeds , or when is not a defined .
Thrown when start and end are equal and is not .

member.name: P:MyOrg.Elements.Range`1.Start
member.summary: Gets the start value of the range.

member.name: P:MyOrg.Elements.Range`1.End
member.summary: Gets the end value of the range.

member.name: P:MyOrg.Elements.Range`1.Type
member.summary: Gets the type of the range.

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Range`1.IsInRange(`0)
member.summary: Indicates whether the specified value is within the range, based on the range type.
member.param: The value to test.
member.returns: if the value is within the range; otherwise .
member.exception: Thrown when is .

example.code: var range = new Range<int>(1, 10); bool atStart = range.IsInRange(1); // true bool atEnd = range.IsInRange(10); // true bool outside = range.IsInRange(11); // false

member.name: T:MyOrg.Elements.RangeType
member.summary: Specifies the type of the range.

member.name: F:MyOrg.Elements.RangeType.Inclusive
member.summary: The range includes both the start and end values.

member.name: F:MyOrg.Elements.RangeType.Exclusive
member.summary: The range excludes both the start and end values.

member.name: F:MyOrg.Elements.RangeType.ExclusiveStart
member.summary: The range excludes the start value but includes the end value.

member.name: F:MyOrg.Elements.RangeType.ExclusiveEnd
member.summary: The range includes the start value but excludes the end value.

member.name: T:MyOrg.Elements.Results.Errors.Error
member.summary: Represents an error that occurred during an operation, including details about the error code, message, and type.
member.remarks: This class is typically used to encapsulate error information in scenarios where operations may fail and detailed error reporting is required. It provides a standardized way to convey error details such as a unique error code, a human-readable message, and the category of the error.

member.name: P:MyOrg.Elements.Results.Errors.Error.Code
member.summary: Gets or sets the error code associated with the error.

member.name: P:MyOrg.Elements.Results.Errors.Error.Message
member.summary: Gets or sets the message associated with the current operation.

member.name: P:MyOrg.Elements.Results.Errors.Error.Exceptions
member.summary: Gets the collection of exceptions that have occurred.
member.remarks: This property can be used to store and retrieve multiple exceptions that may have been encountered during an operation.

member.name: T:MyOrg.Elements.Results.Errors.RuntimeExceptionError
member.summary: Represents an error that occurs due to an unhandled runtime exception.
member.remarks: This error is typically used to encapsulate runtime exceptions that occur during the execution of an application. The instance associated with the runtime error is included in the collection.

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Results.Errors.RuntimeExceptionError.#ctor(System.Exception)
member.summary: Creates a new instance of the class.
member.param: The exception that was encountered.

example.code: var error = new RuntimeExceptionError(caughtException);

member.name: T:MyOrg.Elements.Results.Errors.ValidationError
member.summary: Represents an error that occurs when a validation rule is violated.
member.remarks: This class provides details about the specific property that failed validation, the attempted value that caused the failure, and a descriptive error message.

member.name: P:MyOrg.Elements.Results.Errors.ValidationError.PropertyName
member.summary: Gets the name of the property that failed validation.

member.name: P:MyOrg.Elements.Results.Errors.ValidationError.AttemptedValue
member.summary: Gets the value that was attempted during the operation.

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Results.Errors.ValidationError.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Represents an error that occurs during validation of a specific property.
member.param: The name of the property that caused the validation error.
A message describing the validation error.
The value that was attempted to be set on the property, which caused the validation error.

example.code: var error = new ValidationError(nameof(Order.Quantity), "Must be positive.", "-1");

member.name: T:MyOrg.Elements.Results.Result
member.summary: Represents the outcome of an operation that may either succeed or carry structured errors.

remarks.para: The Result pattern is preferred over exception-based control flow for expected, recoverable failure paths — for example, a resource that does not exist, a validation rule that is violated, or a concurrency conflict on write. Exceptions remain appropriate for truly exceptional, programmer-error conditions (null dereferences, argument violations, etc.); the Result pattern covers the space where throwing would be an abuse of the exception mechanism.

Benefits over try/catch for business logic:
Explicit contracts. The signature Result<Order> immediately communicates that the operation can fail in a structured way, whereas a throwing method forces callers to read documentation or source code to discover which exceptions to handle.
No silent swallowing. A caller that ignores errors is visible — it discards a without inspecting . A caller that swallows an exception is invisible until production.
Multiple errors. Validation routines can accumulate all field errors into a single rather than throwing on the first violation and leaving the caller with incomplete feedback.
Composability. Pipelines and behaviors can inspect, transform, or propagate results without catching and re-throwing, keeping control flow linear and easy to reason about.

For operations that return a value on success, use the generic subclass. The non-generic Result is appropriate for void-style operations (e.g., a delete command) where only the success/failure outcome matters.

member.seealso: Martin Fowler — Replacing Throwing Exceptions with Notification in Validations

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Results.Result.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Results.Result.#ctor(System.Collections.Generic.ICollection{MyOrg.Elements.Results.Errors.Error})
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with a collection of errors.
member.param: The collection of errors associated with the result.

example.code: var result = new Result(new[] { new Error { Code = "NOT_FOUND", Message = "Order not found." } });

member.name: P:MyOrg.Elements.Results.Result.Errors
member.summary: Gets or sets the collection of errors associated with the current operation or context.
member.remarks: Use this property to access or modify the list of errors. Adding or removing items from the collection allows tracking of errors relevant to the operation or context.

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Results.Result.IsSuccess
member.summary: Determines whether the operation completed successfully.
member.returns: if there are no errors; otherwise, .
member.remarks: Implementors may override this behavior to provide custom success logic.

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Results.Result.IsFailure
member.summary: Determines whether the operation failed.
member.returns: if there are any errors; otherwise, .
member.remarks: Implementors may override this behavior to provide custom failure logic.

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Results.Result.Success
member.summary: Creates a successful result with no errors.
member.returns: A successful instance.

example.code: Result result = Result.Success();

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Results.Result.Failure(MyOrg.Elements.Results.Errors.Error)
member.summary: Creates a failed result containing the specified error.
member.param: The error to include in the result.
member.returns: A failed containing the supplied error.

example.code: Result result = Result.Failure(new NotFoundError("Order not found."));

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Results.Result.CreateFailure``1(MyOrg.Elements.Results.Errors.Error[])
member.summary: Creates a failure result of a specific derived Result type.
member.typeparam: The specific Result type to create (must have a parameterless constructor).
member.param: The errors to include in the failure result.
member.returns: A failure result of the specified type.
member.remarks: This method enables behaviors to construct failure results without reflection. Use this when you need to return a specific Result-derived type (e.g., Result<T>, QueryResult<T>).

example.code: var failure = Result.CreateFailure<Result<Order>>(new NotFoundError("Order not found."));

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Results.Result.CreateFailure``1(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{MyOrg.Elements.Results.Errors.Error})
member.summary: Creates a failure result of a specific derived Result type with a collection of errors.
member.typeparam: The specific Result type to create (must have a parameterless constructor).
member.param: The errors to include in the failure result.
member.returns: A failure result of the specified type.

example.code: IEnumerable<Error> errors = validator.Validate(command); var failure = Result.CreateFailure<Result<Order>>(errors);

member.name: T:MyOrg.Elements.Results.Result`1
member.summary: Represents the outcome of an operation that returns a value on success.
member.typeparam: The type of value carried when the operation succeeds.

remarks.para: Use Result<T> in place of returning a bare model value or throwing an exception when the operation may fail for an expected reason. Returning a plain model (or ) forces callers to infer failure from a sentinel value; returning Result<T> makes the possibility of failure explicit in the method signature and provides structured error information without the overhead or control-flow disruption of exception handling.

Prefer Result<T> over raw model returns when:
The operation may legitimately not find a resource (, ).
Input validation may reject the request before any work is done ().
A caller needs to distinguish between a successful empty value and a failure (which a return cannot express).
See for the rationale behind preferring the Result pattern over try/catch for expected failure paths.

member.seealso: Martin Fowler — Replacing Throwing Exceptions with Notification in Validations

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Results.Result`1.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class. Required for compatibility.

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Results.Result`1.#ctor(`0)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with a value.
member.param: The value to carry on success.

example.code: var result = new Result<Order>(order);

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Results.Result`1.#ctor(`0,System.Collections.Generic.ICollection{MyOrg.Elements.Results.Errors.Error})
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with a value and errors.
member.param: The value to carry.
The collection of errors associated with the result.

example.code: var result = new Result<Order>(null, new[] { new Error { Code = "NOT_FOUND", Message = "Order not found." } });

member.name: P:MyOrg.Elements.Results.Result`1.Value
member.summary: Gets the value carried by the result.

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Results.Result`1.Success(`0)
member.summary: Creates a successful result with a value.
member.param: The value to carry.
member.returns: A successful result containing the value.

example.code: Result<Order> ok = Result<Order>.Success(order);

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Results.Result`1.Failure(System.Collections.Generic.ICollection{MyOrg.Elements.Results.Errors.Error})
member.summary: Creates a failed result with multiple errors.
member.param: The collection of errors to include in the result.
member.returns: A failed result containing the errors.

example.code: var bad = Result<Order>.Failure(new[] { new ValidationError("Id", "Required.", "") });

member.name: T:MyOrg.Elements.SystemClock
member.summary: Default implementation that delegates to and .

member.name: T:MyOrg.Elements.Validation.IValidationService
member.summary: Provides the interface for validation services. Validation services are responsible for validating objects and returning the results of the validation.
member.remarks: Validation results should be preferred over throwing exceptions for issues with validation. This allows for a more graceful handling of validation errors, especially in scenarios where multiple validations need to be performed at once. Additionally, this adheres to the general principal of allowing exceptions to be thrown only for truly exceptional cases, rather than for validation errors, which are expected and manageable.

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Validation.IValidationService.Validate``1(``0)
member.summary: Validates the specified object and returns a ValidationResult.
member.typeparam: The type of the object to validate.
member.param: The object to validate.
member.returns: A containing any validation errors that were detected.

member.name: T:MyOrg.Elements.Validation.ValidationError
member.summary: Represents a validation error that occurs during the validation process.
member.param: The error message describing the validation error.
The name of the property that caused the validation error, if applicable.
An optional error code associated with the validation error.

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Validation.ValidationError.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Represents a validation error that occurs during the validation process.
member.param: The error message describing the validation error.
The name of the property that caused the validation error, if applicable.
An optional error code associated with the validation error.

member.name: P:MyOrg.Elements.Validation.ValidationError.PropertyName
member.summary: The name of the property that caused the validation error, if applicable.

member.name: P:MyOrg.Elements.Validation.ValidationError.ErrorMessage
member.summary: The error message describing the validation error.

member.name: P:MyOrg.Elements.Validation.ValidationError.ErrorCode
member.summary: The optional error code associated with the validation error.

member.name: P:MyOrg.Elements.Validation.ValidationError.AttemptedValue
member.summary: The value that was attempted to be set, if applicable.

member.name: T:MyOrg.Elements.Validation.ValidationResult
member.summary: Represents the result of a validation operation, including any validation errors that occurred.

member.name: P:MyOrg.Elements.Validation.ValidationResult.Errors
member.summary: The collection of validation errors that occurred during the validation process.

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Validation.ValidationResult.AddError(MyOrg.Elements.Validation.ValidationError)
member.summary: Adds a validation error to the result.
member.param: The validation error to add.
member.exception: Thrown when error is null.

example.code: var result = new ValidationResult(); result.AddError(new ValidationError("Name is required.", nameof(Customer.Name), "REQUIRED"));

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Validation.ValidationResult.AddError(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Adds a validation error to the result.
member.param: The error message.
The property name that caused the error.
An optional error code.
member.exception: Thrown when errorMessage is null.

example.code: var result = new ValidationResult(); result.AddError("Email is required.", nameof(Customer.Email), "REQUIRED");

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.Validation.ValidationResult.IsValid
member.summary: Indicates whether the validation was successful (i.e., no errors were found).
member.returns: Returns true if the result indicates a successful validation (no errors) or false if the validation failed.

member.name: T:MyOrg.Elements.ValueObject
member.summary: Base class for value objects. Value objects are compared by their structural attributes, not by identity. They should be immutable.
member.remarks: Subclasses must override to enumerate all properties that participate in equality comparison. Two value objects are equal if and only if they have the same type and their equality components are sequence-equal.

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.ValueObject.GetEqualityComponents
member.summary: Gets the components used for equality comparison.
member.returns: An enumerable of property values that define this value object's identity.

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.ValueObject.Equals(MyOrg.Elements.ValueObject)
member.summary: Determines whether this value object equals another value object by comparing equality components.
member.param: The value object to compare to.
member.returns: true if both objects have the same type and equal equality components; otherwise false.

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.ValueObject.op_Equality(MyOrg.Elements.ValueObject,MyOrg.Elements.ValueObject)
member.summary: Determines whether two value objects are equal.
member.param: The left value object.
The right value object.
member.returns: true if the two values are equal; otherwise false.

member.name: M:MyOrg.Elements.ValueObject.op_Inequality(MyOrg.Elements.ValueObject,MyOrg.Elements.ValueObject)
member.summary: Determines whether two value objects are not equal.
member.param: The left value object.
The right value object.
member.returns: true if the two values differ; otherwise false.
